reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [s[0] remains uninitialized, causing undefined behavior when accessed as s[i-1] for i=1]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Reading input into s+1 leaves s[0] uninitialized; later strlen(s+1) may work, but indexing from 1 complicates logic and risks out-of-bounds access when checking s[i+1] near the end]

15.reason: The problem specifies that the character after '-' must be strictly greater than the one before it. Using '<=' incorrectly allows expansion when characters are equal (e.g., 'a-a'), which should remain unchanged.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [condition uses <= but should be < per problem requirement; also accesses s[i-1] when i=1 which is uninitialized]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The problem specifies expansion only when the character after '-' is strictly greater than the one before it, so '<=' should be '<'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The problem specifies that the character after '-' must be strictly greater than the one before it. Using '<=' incorrectly allows expansion when characters are equal (e.g., 'a-a'), which should remain unchanged.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When i is at the last character, s[i+1] accesses beyond the string boundary (undefined behavior). Also, if '-' is at the end (e.g., "a-"), s[i+1] is '\0', which fails type check but still causes invalid read]

13.reason: [C89 does not allow declaration in for loop initialization; declare int i; at start of block]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [C89 does not allow declaration in for loop initialization; declare int i; at start of block]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  strlen() is called in every iteration causing O(N^2) complexity; comparing int with size_t may cause compilation warnings/errors in strict environments.

25.reason: [C89 does not allow declaration in for loop initialization; declare char j; at start of block]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [C89 does not allow declaration in for loop initialization; declare char j; at start of block]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Using 'char' for loop variable j can cause overflow and infinite loop if s[pos-1] is 127 (max signed char); should use 'int'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If expansion range is large (e.g., 'a' to 'z'), this loop works, but if s[i] > s[pos-1] (shouldn't happen due to earlier check), it could loop infinitely or incorrectly. However, main issue is that s[i] might not be the start of the range — e.g., in "a-c-e", overlapping marks may cause wrong expansion]
